summary: Iteration 141: Balance Calculation Double-Counting Bug Fix - VERIFIED. The critical bug where type='profit' entries in deposits collection caused ~$8,000 inflation has been fixed. Account value now correctly shows $33,664.09 instead of the buggy $41,664.94.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix correctly excludes type='profit' entries from deposits in /api/profit/daily-balances (line 2079-2082)
Frontend ProfitTrackerPage.jsx correctly filters type='profit' from onlyDeposits (line 1134-1136)
Frontend correctly filters type='profit' in simulated view deposits (line 1070)
seed_data_creation: No new seed data needed - tested with existing production data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Balance calculation bug fix verified. The deposits collection contains type='profit' entries that were previously double-counted. Backend now excludes them via d.get('type') != 'profit' checks. Expected values: Account Value = $33,664.09, Total Profit Deposits Excluded = $8,000.85. February 2026 has $3,000 withdrawal on Feb 18 correctly reflected in Feb 19 balance.

verified_values.february_withdrawal: $3,000 on Feb 18 correctly affects Feb 19 balance

ui_elements_verified: Monthly Table > History section shows correct trade data
Daily Projection modal shows Balance Before column with correct values

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The deposits collection stores both real deposits AND type='profit' entries (duplicates of trade_logs). The backend /api/profit/daily-balances endpoint was counting profit-type deposits as regular deposits AND also counting them from trade_logs, causing double-counting.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Added d.get('type') != 'profit' filter in backend server.py (lines 2079-2082, 2248-2250) and frontend ProfitTrackerPage.jsx (lines 1070, 1134-1136)
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 9 backend tests pass. Frontend displays correct Account Value of $33,664.09. Debug endpoint shows $8,000.85 in total_profit_deposits_excluded field.
